rag_findings.detail: The architectural style, specifically the white-washed buildings with flat concrete overhangs and the prevalence of unfinished concrete block perimeter walls with wire-mesh fencing, is highly characteristic of rural Albania. Specialist evidence strongly supports Albania over Turkey based on the specific combination of these structural elements and the landscape.

The architectural style, specifically the unfinished concrete block construction and the specific way the house is built with an open upper floor, is more characteristic of the Western Balkans (North Macedonia) than rural Greece. While both are plausible, the meta and landscape evidence suggests a stronger alignment with the Balkan construction patterns seen in North Macedonia.

The architectural style, specifically the unfinished concrete block walls and the white-washed buildings with flat concrete overhangs, is strongly characteristic of rural Albania. The combination of Mediterranean vegetation and the specific style of wire-mesh fencing on low concrete bases further supports this identification over North Macedonia.
